task: Replay one owner-approved LLM topic decision as the Scout handoff for the no-write worker chain.
prompt: This is a deterministic replay artifact created from a human-approved topic decision. No live Scout rerank is performed.

proposals.title: GSC opportunity review: Last Z Research Guide — Best Research Order, Peace Shield, and T10 Path
proposals.source_reference: GSC weekly 2026-05-03: page opportunity and query-page signals
proposals.notes: research.html shows meaningful impressions but middling CTR, plus a rising research priority query. This suggests the page likely needs clearer positioning of order, scope, and progression value rather than new content.
proposals.player_value: Players get a more useful research path and faster understanding of what to prioritize first.
proposals.duplication_risk: Medium; must stay distinct from costs, hero training, and peace shield guidance.
proposals.next_step: Route to human review to test whether the page can better answer research priority intent within current structure.
evidence: GSC page signal: research.html had 12710 impressions, 597 clicks, 4.70% CTR, avg position 7.27.
Rising query: `last z research priority` gained 11 impressions in the last 7-day comparison window.

site_fit.primary_user_job: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for `last z research guide` searchers.
site_fit.archetype_reason: Existing page is `cornerstone-guide` in the `Research` cluster; Scout should prefer updating the existing page before proposing new content.

constraints: Use existing page template and navigation patterns.
Treat GSC data as a signal, not proof that a rewrite is needed.
reject_if: The query intent is already better served by another canonical page.
The proposed change would blur cluster role separation.
The improvement cannot be expressed without changing a cornerstone page beyond the approved scope.

guardrails: Only approved_for_chain decisions may be replayed.
Replay does not approve public copy, patch specs, backlog edits, manifests, PRs, or deployment.
Owner approval remains required before any user-visible content change.
